Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jun-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS-DEFENDANT BRYAN CAVE LLP'S Motion To Approve Good Faith Settlement is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint Against Cameron Smith And Bryan Cave Llp
Off Calendar May-08-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. GUY D. CALLADINE OF THE CARLSON, CALLADINE & PETERSON LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-391-8140 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. AMIR KALTGARD OF LINER LLP, TEL. NO. 1-310-500-3500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Liner LLP's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint against Cameron Smith and Bryan Cave LLP is granted. Granting the motion is in accord with California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings and deciding cases on their merits. The cross-complaint against Mr. Smith is compulsory and, if not allowed, will be lost forever. While it is possible, perhaps even likely, that Liner had sufficient information to file a cross-complaint at the time it filed its answer, that does not mean this motion is brought in bad faith or otherwise is unfair or prejudicial to plaintiffs to Bryan Cave. There is an insufficient showing that Line brought this motion in bad faith. Nor is there a sufficient showing that the cross-claim against Mr. Smith funs afoul of any of the public policy concerns addressed in the cases cited on pages 10 and 11 of plaintiffs' opposition memorandum. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File Cross-Complaint Against Cameron Smith And Bryan Cave Llp

Defendant Liner LLP's motion for leave to filed cross-complaint against Cameron Smith and Bryan Cave LLP is continued to May 8, 2017, at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, per the agreement of the parties. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =(302/HK)
